hypothesis: A hybrid adaptive batching algorithm that dynamically switches between static-64 and dynamic batching strategies based on a real-time estimate of the current graph-size variance in the training buffer will achieve greater training speedup than either algorithm alone across datasets with non-stationary graph-size distributions, because exploiting the compilation efficiency of static-64 during low-variance phases while avoiding costly recompilations during high-variance phases will reduce total combined training time by more than the overhead introduced by the switching mechanism itself.
